entries.rationale: These are shell-level labels around an already governed checkout flow. They remain temporarily local so the phase can stabilize baseline behavior before label normalization work.
Runtime fallback wording still exists in the loading bridge even though SSR data attributes are present. Keep it reviewable as temporary migration debt until runtime fallback removal is scheduled.

matchValues: Please review the highlighted checkout details.
entries.rationale: Checkout runtime currently carries mirrored fallback copy for validation and loading. This is known baseline debt and should not drown out future governance regressions.

matchValues: Enter your apartment, suite, or unit if needed.
Confirm the required checkbox to continue.
entries.rationale: Checkout field-level validation remains runtime-local for now. This is documented runtime governance debt and should only resurface when it changes or escalates.

matchValues: We could not update the bag just now. Please try again.
entries.rationale: Add-to-cart runtime shares the same inline fallback as the cart drawer flow. It is a known baseline debt and should collapse into the governed runtime path later.

matchValues: Select an option before adding to bag.
entries.rationale: Variation-selection feedback is still runtime-local while the rest of the cart feedback family moves through governed SSR bridges. Keep it visible as deferred migration debt.

matchValues: A secure password will be prepared and sent after registration.
entries.rationale: WooCommerce account registration copy still falls back locally in the auth template. This is unresolved legacy surface debt and remains deferred until account auth consolidation.

matchValues: Use the details below for a smoother return to checkout next time.
entries.rationale: The address form still includes a local fallback even though the governed account surface exists. This is accepted as temporary migration debt, not as silent bypass.
Recovery controls remain inline for compatibility with WooCommerce account templates. They are documented structural exceptions and must be revisited during account label normalization.
Password form labels are structural account controls, not tonal helper copy. They are accepted into the baseline as reviewable label exceptions until a dedicated normalization phase exists.
Auth-shell labels and recovery links remain inline by design in this phase. They are documented exceptions, not hidden bypasses.
Address-form headings are low-risk structural labels. Keep them baseline-documented until account label normalization is explicitly scheduled.
Reset-password heading is a structural action label. It stays reviewable in the baseline rather than being silently ignored.
Search retry wording is a small local interaction label. It is documented as deferred discovery debt rather than treated as a fresh regression.
Search trigger labels are structural discovery controls. They are reviewable exceptions until label normalization broadens into search IA work.
